original_abstract: Background: We address the problem of selecting and assessing classification and regression models using cross-validation. Current state-of-the-art methods can yield models with high variance, rendering them unsuitable for a number of practical applications including QSAR. In this paper we describe and evaluate best practices which improve reliability and increase confidence in selected models. A key operational component of the proposed
humanized_abstract: The aim of this paper is to investigate the problem of choosing classifiers and regression models with low variance using cross-validation for being applicable to QSAR. State-of-the-art methods that were recently proposed can lead to highly variant models which cannot be used in many situations. The best practices to improve the reliability of model choice and thereby to obtain more reliable models is presented and examined. An essential element of the proposed strategies is cross-validation which appears to be a very efficient tool in this context.
